name: Rembrandt Circuit
description: Cycle the moderate 31.1-mile Rembrandt Circuit, a touring route through French countryside, featuring a Rembrandt painting in Le Mas-d'Agena
region_intro: Touring cycling around Le Mas-D'Agenais features routes primarily along the Canal Latéral à la Garonne and the Garonne river, characterized by flat or gently rolling terrain. The landscape includes waterways, agricultural areas, and small villages, providing a consistent surface for touring cyclists. Elevation changes are generally modest across the region's routes. Best touring cycling routes around Le Mas-D'Agenais The most popular touring cycling route is Rembrandt Circuit, a moderate 31.1 miles (50.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours to complete. This route offers a balanced touring experience through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Canal Latéral à la Garonne – Baïse Aqueduct loop from Le Mas-d'Agenais, a difficult 66.2 miles (106.5 km) path. It follows the Canal Latéral à la Garonne, featuring the Baïse Aqueduct. Local touring cyclists also love the Garonne Lateral Canal – Canal Latéral à la Garonne loop from Caumont-sur-Garonne, a 26.0 miles (41.9 km) trail leading through the Garonne Lateral Canal area, often completed in about 2 hours 25 minutes. Touring cycling around Le Mas-D'Agenais is defined by canal paths, riverbanks, and rural roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy touring bike trails to more challenging long distance bike routes. The routes in Le Mas-D'Agenais are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 240 reviews. More than 1600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Le Mas-D'Agenais's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Le Mas-D'Agenais?
A: There are over 65 touring cycling routes around Le Mas-D'Agenais, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy, short rides to more challenging long-distance tours.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in this region?
A: The touring cycling routes around Le Mas-D'Agenais are primarily characterized by flat or gently rolling terrain. Many paths follow the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1043134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Latéral à la Garonne&lt;/a&gt; and the Garonne river, providing consistent surfaces through waterways, agricultural areas, and small villages. Significant elevation changes are rare.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 24 easy touring cycling routes in the area. A good option for a relaxed ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24831688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduct of the Avance loop from Caumont-sur-Garonne&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 12.7 km long and takes less than an hour to complete. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41466178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne Lateral Canal – Le Mas-d'Agenais loop from Caumont-sur-Garonne&lt;/a&gt;, covering 25.9 km.

Q: What are some longer touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a longer challenge, the region offers 10 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37574650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Latéral à la Garonne – Baïse Aqueduct loop from Le Mas-d'Agenais&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 106.5 km path that follows the Canal Latéral à la Garonne and features the impressive Baïse Aqueduct.

Q: What are the best seasons for touring cycling around Le Mas-D'Agenais?
A: The region's generally flat terrain and consistent surfaces make it suitable for touring cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, but routes along the canals and rivers often provide shade and refreshing breezes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes around Le Mas-D'Agenais are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287241957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rembrandt Circuit&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37575502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garonne Lateral Canal – Canal Latéral à la Garonne loop from Caumont-sur-Garonne&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by historical and natural points of interest. You can cycle alongside the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1043134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Latéral à la Garonne&lt;/a&gt;, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4519234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Vincent Church in Le Mas-d’Agenais&lt;/a&gt; to see Rembrandt’s Christ on the Cross, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6073790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibra Mill&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5791314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;So Vélo Greenway&lt;/a&gt; also offers a dedicated path for cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Le Mas-D'Agenais?
A: The touring cycling routes in Le Mas-D'Agenais are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 240 reviews. Cyclists often praise the well-maintained canal paths, the peaceful rural scenery, and the variety of routes suitable for different abilities.

Q: Is public transport available to access the cycling routes?
A: While specific public transport connections directly to trailheads might vary, Le Mas-D'Agenais is generally accessible by regional transport. Many routes are easily reached from the town center or nearby villages, which may have bus services. It's advisable to check local transport schedules for the most up-to-date information on carrying bikes.

Q: Are there places to park my car when starting a touring cycling route?
A: Yes, you can typically find parking in Le Mas-D'Agenais and the surrounding villages like Caumont-sur-Garonne, which serve as common starting points for many routes. Look for designated parking areas near the canal or town centers.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Yes, many routes pass through or near small villages and towns along the Garonne river and Canal Latéral à la Garonne. These often have local cafes, bakeries, or small shops where you can stop for refreshments and a break.
tips:
Rembrandt, born July 15, 1606, died October 4, 1669 is
considered one of the greatest painters in the history of art
European Baroque and one of the most important painters of the Dutch School of the 17th century. He produced more than 400 paintings, 300 etchings and 300 drawings. In 1631, he painted Christ on the Cross which is visible in the St-Vincent collegiate church of Mas-d’Agenais. From the paved square with a medieval-looking market hall, you must enter the 12th century Collegiate Church, get lost between sarcophagi and sculpted stalls, before discovering “The Crucifixion”. The painting is part of a series by the master on the Passion of Christ.

In the lower plain of the Garonne, the Etang de la Mazière nature reserve protects 68 ha of wetlands within landscapes characteristic of intensive agriculture. Formed by an old arm of the river, the pond is made up of a mosaic of environments (pond, damp wood, reed bed, gravel pits, pastured wasteland) which constitute an attraction for birds and aquatic fauna.
The lock of the Gaulle is the number 42 of the side canal of the Garonne. The jump is more than two meters and the setting is quite nice!
A large shaded area with picnic facilities and stunning views of the river, canal and bridge.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Rembrandt Circuit?
faqs.answer: The Komoot estimate for cycling the Rembrandt Circuit is approximately 3 hours. This is for the 50.8-kilometer route, but your actual time may vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or visit points of interest.

faqs.question: Is the Rembrandt Circuit suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Rembrandt Circuit is categorized as moderate. While it includes relatively flat sections, especially near the Canal de Garonne, it also features 'a few bumps' or rolling hills as it ventures into the surrounding countryside. Beginners with a reasonable level of fitness should find it manageable, but be prepared for some gentle ascents.

faqs.question: Where does the Rembrandt Circuit start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular Rembrandt Circuit begins and ends in Le Mas d'Agenais. Parking is generally available in the village, which serves as a convenient starting point for the route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Rembrandt Circuit?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite diverse. You'll experience picturesque, relatively flat sections along the Canal de Garonne. As the route moves away from the canal, it transitions into rolling hills and gentle ascents through rich farmland, varied countryside, and charming villages.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Rembrandt Circuit?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during various seasons. However, if you plan to ride during the off-season, be aware that shops in the less-touristy rural areas may have limited opening hours. It's advisable to carry your own snacks and drinks during these times.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The most remarkable highlight is the original Rembrandt painting, Christ on the Cross (1631), located in the Rembrandt’s Christ on the Cross in Saint-Vincent Church, Le Mas-d’Agenais. Other notable spots include the Mazière Pond Nature Reserve and the Écluse de la Gaule. The route also offers diverse scenery of farmland, countryside, and charming villages.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities along the Rembrandt Circuit?
faqs.answer: The route passes through charming villages and small towns where you may find amenities. However, if you are cycling during the off-season, be prepared for limited opening hours for shops and cafes in the more rural areas. It's a good idea to carry your own snacks and drinks just in case.

faqs.question: Does the Rembrandt Circuit intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route links into other nearby cycling routes, offering possibilities for extended exploration. Specifically, it covers sections of the EV3 France 42, Langon - Buzet-sur-Baïse, and Le Canal de la Garonne à vélo: Marmande / Damazan.

faqs.question: Is the Rembrandt Circuit dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a touring bicycle route primarily on roads and dedicated paths, dogs are generally permitted if they can safely accompany you, for example, in a bike trailer or basket. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local regulations regarding pets in villages and nature areas.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle the Rembrandt Circuit?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Rembrandt Circuit. It is a publicly accessible route through the French countryside.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a day trip on the Rembrandt Circuit?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and potential for limited amenities in off-season, it's wise to pack sufficient water, snacks, and basic bike repair tools. Sun protection, appropriate cycling attire, and a camera for the scenic views and the Rembrandt painting are also recommended.

waypoints.name: Rembrandt’s Christ on the Cross in Saint-Vincent Church, Le Mas-d’Agenais
